Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Nice to see this laid to rest. Someone somewhere might still be using this. It might be useful to land a warning message when snmp support is enabled before 2.16 drops, then remove snmp immediately after. But the code is in stasis and doesn't seem to inter-depend much on other parts of Lustre. So older versions of this probably still work fine with newer Lustre. At least, I doubt they're more broken.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> Nice to see this laid to rest. Someone somewhere might still be using this. It might be useful to land a warning message when snmp support is enabled before 2.16 drops, then remove snmp immediately after. But the code is in stasis and doesn't seem to inter-depend much on other parts of Lustre. So older versions of this probably still work fine with newer Lustre. At least, I doubt they're more broken.

Normally I'd be in favor of deprecation - and I'm happy to do so if Andreas thinks it's appropriate - but this code has been adjusted only once in the last 11 years and just twice in the last 15, so it seems highly likely it's already unused.  (Andreas?)
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> > 
> > Nice to see this laid to rest. Someone somewhere might still be using this. It might be useful to land a warning message when snmp support is enabled before 2.16 drops, then remove snmp immediately after. But the code is in stasis and doesn't seem to inter-depend much on other parts of Lustre. So older versions of this probably still work fine with newer Lustre. At least, I doubt they're more broken.
> 
> Normally I'd be in favor of deprecation - and I'm happy to do so if Andreas thinks it's appropriate - but this code has been adjusted only once in the last 11 years and just twice in the last 15, so it seems highly likely it's already unused.  (Andreas?)

FWIW, I strongly suspect large parts of this don't work any more, if any of it works at all.  Our exact stats and status stuff does drift over time and this hasn't been kept up to date with it.

parents.subject: LU-9859 libcfs: move kernel specific code out of libcfs core

commit.subject: LU-17065 build: Remove snmp support
commit.message:
LU-17065 build: Remove snmp support

Last patched in 2012 and not well tended to before that,
snmp support can probably be removed.
